Description: Cylindrical body — Armenian Duduk A3 root
Notes: Apricot: specialty source req. Walnut: Woodcraft or similar. +2.75" over est. body for chuck allowance.

Description: Giant cane double reed for A3 duduk
Notes: Buy 4–6 for initial testing. Reeds break during fitting/adjustment. Source from Armenian music shops.

Material: Synthetic or natural fiber cord / heat-shrink tubing
Notes: Traditional: wound thread. Modern: heat-shrink or rubber band. Qty 2: one fitted + one spare.

Description: Food-safe bore oil (linseed or walnut oil)
Material: Linseed oil (boiled) or raw walnut oil
Notes: Thin coats; flood bore and drain. Let cure 48 hrs between coats. 3 coats minimum.

Material: Pure tung oil or beeswax + carnauba blend
Notes: Do not use film-forming finish on bore. Exterior: 2–3 coats wipe-on.

Description: Thread or tape to fit reed shank into socket
Material: PTFE plumber's tape or linen thread
Notes: Wrap reed shank to achieve airtight fit in socket. Adjust wrap thickness for tuning.
Wrap around a dowel slightly smaller than bore. Work progressively finer.

Description: Layout dye or pencil for hole center marking
Notes: Mark hole centers from drawing. Verify with caliper before drilling.

column12: All costs USD estimated; verify current pricing before ordering.

Description: Reed cost dominates BOM. Quality reeds from Armenian suppliers may be $30–100 each.
Apricot wood: if unavailable locally, consider Craft Supplies USA or specialist turning-blank suppliers.
All costs are estimates only — verify before purchasing. Last checked: 2026-05-08.
